This opportunity record was created 2026-07-06 with a blank LastActivityDate, while the verified Avoma buyer history for the Omnia account runs from April 2025 through 30 Jun 2026; whether this specific record is the correct home for that history could not be confirmed.
items.source: ground truth + Avoma manifest cross-check, this sweep

sources: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
metrics.narrative: Omnia's stated business problem is a fully manual, Excel-based Source-to-Contract process covering roughly ZAR 16 billion (~$1B) of spend. Marianne Ventura quantified the pain directly: "I sit today with 15 sourcing events, guys, across varying categories, and I have 18 contracts in flight, of which I've got, like, call it 11 that I'm being niggled about" (Omnia_Zycus Alignment for 13/10 presentation call, 8 Oct 2025). A separate buyer voice on the SOW closure call (30 Jun 2026) flagged a recurring three-week delay receiving spend data after month-close as "a significant frustration," citing a live request they could not action fast enough. No separate ROI/savings figure has been confirmed.

sources: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
champion.narrative: Marianne Ventura has personally driven the deal since at least October 2025, framing the executive presentation around securing sign-off ("the end result to this is I'm asking them to give me the money for the system"), personally negotiating final commercial terms (raising the spend threshold, adjusting country phasing) on 30 Jun 2026, and closing that call by telling the Zycus team "we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign."

sources: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
competition.narrative: The only named competitor across 14 months of calls is the incumbent Adobe Sign, criticized for weak workflow integration and 60% adoption (Ashen Chathury, 24 Apr 2025); Certinal was accepted in its place and reconfirmed in the final SOW on 30 Jun 2026. One early call referenced Omnia "exploring a source-to-contract system and considering Zycus among other options" (Ashen Chathury, 24 Apr 2025), but no alternative S2C vendor was ever named and the deal proceeded straight into Zycus contracting.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated: manual, Excel-governed sourcing and contracting, inability to see supplier sanctions exposure without manual research, and an incumbent e-signature tool (Adobe Sign) with "a lack of workflow integration and a 60% adoption rate" (Ashen Chathury, Certinal and Omnia Holdings, 24 Apr 2025).

sources: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
paper_process.narrative: The paper trail is explicit: an MSA plus two Order Forms plus a Statement of Work, with Certinal confirmed as the e-signature method. On the 30 Jun 2026 call, Marianne Ventura described Omnia's own signing sequence: internal legal-cover sign-off via Adobe Sign for audit trail, then routing to the relevant stakeholder Delivery for countersignature, saying she needed "one more day" to send the cleaned SOW and that Omnia had "gotta go in a couple of days" to report they are ready to sign.

sources: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
economic_buyer.narrative: In the October 2025 executive alignment call, Marianne Ventura named an unnamed Omnia legal director/board member ("one of my sponsors... he also happens to be one of the directors of Omnia") alongside Jacques de Villiers (Head of Supply Chain, per Salesforce) as the two whose sign-off was "crucial for budget sign-off" (8 Oct 2025). From March through June 2026, every SOW and contracting conversation has run at the Marianne Ventura / Dieter Sparbier / Tracy Seeley-Jeppe working level with no confirmed direct engagement from the named director or Jacques de Villiers in the final commercial close. The economic buyer role is identified but not evidenced as engaged in the actual signature step.

sources: Avoma: FW: Source to Contract Demo and Business Case, 2025-10-13
Avoma: Zycus with Omnia: Modules Walkthrough Session, 2026-03-18
Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
decision_process.narrative: The process moved from an executive business-case presentation (13 Oct 2025) through detailed design-document walkthroughs (18 Mar 2026), technical onboarding (7 Apr 2026), and structured SOW redlining sessions (17, 23, 30 Jun 2026), where Marianne Ventura, with Tracy Seeley-Jeppe and legal, reviewed and resolved comment threads line by line before accepting final terms on 30 Jun 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria were built out in detail across the workshops: a risk dashboard flagging supplier sanctions and geographic exposure without manual research (8 Oct 2025), end-to-end contract lifecycle management with boilerplate-clause assistance, and event-based integration as the preferred pattern with API as fallback (Dieter Sparbier, Zycus - OMNIA - SOW review, 23 Jun 2026). Omnia also required that functionality shown in demos be explicitly captured and enforceable in the contract rather than left to marketing description (SOW alignment call, 17 Jun 2026).

items.name: Morning catch-up (no recording available)
items.summary: A brief internal catch-up ahead of the afternoon SOW closure session; content not captured.

items.name: SOW discussion and closure call
items.summary: Every outstanding SOW redline resolved; spend threshold raised to ZAR 1B; Indonesia and DR Congo dropped from initial scope; Certinal confirmed for e-signature; buyer described her internal sign-off process and committed to routing documents to Zycus for countersignature within days.

day_summary.overall: The last day with real activity was 30 Jun 2026 (about nine days before this sweep). The buyer's champion, Marianne Ventura, worked through every remaining SOW comment with Zycus, closed them all out, confirmed Certinal as the e-signature provider, finalized the country-phasing and spend-threshold changes, and described her own internal legal sign-off process before routing the MSA, Order Form, and SOW to Zycus for countersignature. She told the Zycus team she needed to report internally within days that the deal was ready to sign. No activity has been logged in the nine days since.

cro_panel.intro: The paper trail for this deal reads as far more advanced than its recorded stage suggests; the statement of work was fully redlined and closed out nine days ago, with the buyer describing her own internal signature routing and a self-imposed deadline to report 'ready to sign.' The open question is not deal health but whether that routing actually completed, since nothing has followed in the nine days since

contributions.detail: Stage/forecast inflation: 'Vendor Selected' + 'Best Case' stamped on a record created 2 days ago with zero activity and a close date 5 years out — a material data-integrity and forecast-accuracy risk.
Single-threaded relationship — champion Marianne Ventura carrying the deal alone, with no second buyer-side contact or executive access evidenced.

Multiple open scoping items unresolved: sanctions-screening parity (World-Check One vs. DNB/Refinitiv), implementation-partner ambiguity raised twice, and ERP fragmentation forcing Indonesia/DRC out of rollout scope.
No live competitive threat — Adobe Sign is dormant/being actively displaced, and no rival has resurfaced since an April 2025 mention, which caps downside on competitive loss.

deal_movement.summary: This account relationship has run a full enterprise sales motion: e-signature discovery (Apr 2025), executive business-case demo (Oct 2025), deep module walkthroughs and technical onboarding (Mar-Apr 2026), and SOW negotiation to closure (Jun 2026). The buyer accepted final terms on 30 Jun 2026 and said she would report readiness to sign within days; no touchpoint since then confirms whether that happened.

forecast_read.math: days_to_close = 1,819 per the recorded 2031-07-01 close date vs. today (2026-07-08). This figure is not usable for pacing: the underlying evidence places the deal at signature-imminent status, not five years from close.
forecast_read.reason: The recorded close date (2031-07-01, 1,819 days out) reads as a five-year contract-term-end date rather than a sales close date. Real call evidence shows the Statement of Work was fully negotiated and accepted by the buyer on 30 Jun 2026 (about 8 days before this sweep), with the buyer's side actively routing it for internal legal sign-off ahead of countersignature. This is a date/number problem, not a deal-health problem, but until the close date is corrected and signature status confirmed, the Best Case forecast cannot be read against a credible timeline.

items.detail: The stage is recorded as Vendor Selected in Salesforce, but the deal was created 2 days ago with zero activity, zero stakeholders, and a 5-year-out close date. This stage may be incorrect or the deal may be a placeholder. If the stage is real, the absence of any post-selection activity (contracting, legal review, MSA negotiation) and the extreme close-date horizon suggest the selection is not yet operationalized.

Close date is 2031-07-01 (1,819 days out, 5 years). For a Vendor Selected deal with Best Case forecast, a 60-90 day close timeline is typical. The 5-year horizon is either a data-entry error or encodes a contract-end milestone rather than a signature target. This makes all velocity and urgency math meaningless.

No economic buyer, no executive sponsor, no CFO/procurement-director engagement is documented. Zero contact roles exist. For a $234k renewal at an enterprise account, exec alignment is required to secure budget and navigate procurement/legal, but it is completely unmapped.

No champion, no internal advocate, no buying-committee map exists. The deal is zero-threaded. Without a champion to drive the renewal, defend the budget, and navigate internal approvals, the deal is at high risk of stalling or being deprioritized.

The known-topics note references detailed prior activity (material-breach negotiation resolved 17 Jun 2026, sanctions-screening scoping deferred to design phase, implementation-partner ambiguity, legacy-ERP fragmentation scoping finalized 30 Jun 2026, and named stakeholders Marianne Ventura, Jacques de Villiers, Tracy Seeley-Jeppe, Dieter Sparbier, Zama Khambula, Ashen Chathury), but this opportunity (006P700000aX2mH) was created 2026-07-06 with zero calls or tasks. This suggests either (a) the known-topics history belongs to a DIFFERENT opportunity and was incorrectly associated with this one, or (b) this opp_id is a new placeholder for a prior engagement that was tracked elsewhere. Either way, the known-topics evidence is not traceable to this opp_id in the current sweep, creating a provenance gap.

Whether an external implementation partner is needed (vs. Zycus direct plus an Omnia-hired PM/change manager) has surfaced twice; Zycus recommended proceeding without a partner but the decision is not finalized.

This opportunity record shows zero logged contact roles and no logged activity despite verified engagement as recently as 8 days before this sweep, making the deal's true state invisible without cross-referencing Avoma.

critical_signals.text: In the SOW closure call on 30 June 2026, Omnia's buying team accepted the final commercial and scope terms (spend threshold raised, country phasing finalized, Certinal confirmed as e-signature) and said they need to report back within days that they are ready to sign.
The statement of work is fully negotiated. Omnia's side described completing internal legal sign-off before routing the contract to Zycus for countersignature, the last step before close.
Omnia asked Zycus to confirm whether Dun & Bradstreet or Refinitiv can replace their current sanctions-screening tool before that integration is finalized in the design phase.
The incumbent e-signature tool, Adobe Sign, has already been displaced in scope by Certinal, with no other vendor competing for this deal.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement is real, recent, and advancing toward signature; the uncertainty in this record is administrative (contact roles, close date, activity logging), not commercial.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 1819 (5 years out). Created 2026-07-06, last modified 2026-07-06, no LastActivityDate on record. Time in Vendor Selected: 2 days. This is not a real 2031 renewal; the close date and stage reflect a data-entry error or a placeholder. A Vendor Selected deal with Best Case forecast typically closes within 60-90 days, not 1,819. Forward slip: not applicable (no prior close-date history). Pace required: cannot be computed against an indefensible 2031 target.
evidence: Deal created 2026-07-06, stage Vendor Selected, forecast Best Case, close date 2031-07-01 (5 years out).
No LastActivityDate, no Next_Step__c, no field history beyond creation.
Zero tasks, zero events, zero contact roles on record.

The opp name ('OmniaS2C24 Contract Renewal 7/1/2031') suggests a 2024 contract cycle with a 2031 end date, but the deal was created in July 2026 with no prior activity, implying the name may encode a future contract-expiration milestone rather than an active close date.
Best Case forecast with Vendor Selected stage and a 5-year horizon is internally contradictory and not defensible.
north_star_verdict.headline: Vendor-selected deal created 2 days ago with a 5-year-out close date (Jul 2031) is a data-quality anomaly requiring immediate correction: the stage, forecast, and close date are misaligned with reality, and the deal shows zero buyer activity since creation.

flags: Confirm signature status now: Marianne expected to route for signature within days of the 30 Jun 2026 closure, and a week has passed with no confirming evidence - close this loop before the board-reporting urgency she cited fades.
Deliver the two outstanding Zycus commitments (D&B pricing/MSA docs email, World-Check vs DNB compliance-fit read) promptly so the SOW does not get reopened over an unresolved scoping item.

Lock in the change-management/partner-role workshop early in configuration kickoff to prevent the local-partner ambiguity (raised in both Oct 2025 and Jun 2026) from resurfacing as implementation risk.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is functionally at signature; the highest-leverage next steps are verification and record hygiene, not selling.

deal_risk.text: This opportunity record carries zero logged contacts and zero logged activity despite 15 months of real engagement, and the close date (1 Jul 2031) cannot be used for any forecasting math — a record-integrity issue that could make a signature-ready deal invisible to reporting.

The sanctions/compliance-screening tool decision (World-Check One vs DNB/Refinitiv/iOxcio) and the implementation-partner role are both still unresolved and deferred to the design phase, which could resurface after signature.

win_position.text: The statement of work was fully closed out on 30 Jun 2026, with country phasing, spend threshold, supplier-master model and e-signature provider all agreed and no live competitor anywhere in 15 months of engagement.
The buyer's champion (Marianne Ventura) has personally driven every stage of contracting and explicitly committed to routing signed documents to Zycus within days of the 30 Jun 2026 call.
No confirmation exists that the internal legal sign-off or document routing the buyer promised for early July 2026 actually happened; the deal has gone silent at exactly the highest-stakes moment.

Direct engagement from the economic buyer (the board-level legal director) and the other named approver, Jacques de Villiers, has not been seen since 8 Oct 2025 — nine months ago — leaving budget sign-off unconfirmed even though the spend threshold was raised without pushback.

deal_momentum.text: Ten substantive buyer touchpoints between Oct 2025 and Jun 2026 show steady, deepening engagement culminating in a full SOW closure call on 30 Jun 2026.
No buyer or rep activity is recorded in the nine days since the 30 Jun 2026 closure call, right when the buyer said she would report back within days.

customer_commitment.text: Marianne Ventura personally coordinated legal, IT integration (Dieter Sparbier), and financial-systems (Zama Khambula) stakeholders and told the Zycus team on 30 Jun 2026 that 'we've gotta go in a couple of days... we have to report we're ready to sign.'

items.quote: Omnia requires the physical functionality demonstrated (e.g., iContract end-to-end workflow, auto-population, signature flow, contract register) to be explicitly captured and enforceable in contractual documents to avoid being locked into a 5-year license if deployment fails.
items.said_by: unnamed Omnia legal director/board member (via SOW alignment discussion)
items.requirement: Explicitly capture demonstrated iContract functionality in binding contract documents as material breach.

items.quote: World-Check One delivers sanctions, political exposure and regulatory findings that Omnia needs... assess whether DNB/Refinitiv (or iOxcio) can cover the same fields and scope integration accordingly.
items.requirement: Assess whether DNB or Refinitiv can replace World-Check One's sanctions/political-exposure screening.

items.quote: Implementation roles: Zycus will perform majority of configuration, design, UAT/training and will provide PM/PMO; an external partner is optional and primarily valuable for local change management presence in South Africa.
items.requirement: Clarify overlapping roles/responsibilities between Zycus and the proposed implementation partner.

items.quote: There is a delay of three weeks in receiving spend data after the monthly close, which is a significant frustration... We need our data quicker than that.
items.requirement: Provide spend data faster than the current 3-week post-month-close delay.

items.commitment: Email the Dun & Bradstreet enrichment pricing ($10-25k/year) and the two updated MSA/Order Form documents.
items.deliverable: Email the Dun & Bradstreet enrichment pricing ($10-25k/year) and the two updated MSA/Order Form documents.
items.grounding_quote: I'll put it in an email, and then put also the two new MSA in order form. So please only send the last ones.

items.source: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
items.commitment: Check with the DNB partner team on South Africa-specific data coverage versus World-Check One.
items.deliverable: Check with the DNB partner team on South Africa-specific data coverage versus World-Check One.
items.grounding_quote: We can check with the DNB partner team.

items.source: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
items.commitment: Schedule a dedicated change-management capability workshop for the Omnia team during configuration.
items.deliverable: Schedule a dedicated change-management capability workshop for the Omnia team during configuration.
items.grounding_quote: We are gonna go through the configuration workshops and all, maybe have a dedicated workshop or a session where we can tell the team or demonstrate what all we can do in the change management.

items.source: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30
items.commitment: Complete internal legal cover sign-off and route the SOW/MSA/Order Forms to Zycus for countersignature.
items.deliverable: Complete internal legal cover sign-off and route the SOW/MSA/Order Forms to Zycus for countersignature.
items.grounding_quote: I need one more day, and then I will send it through... we've gotta go in a couple of days, and we have to report we're ready to sign.

items.commitment: Share Omnia's integration standards/framework document with Zycus.
items.deliverable: Share Omnia's integration standards/framework document with Zycus.
items.grounding_quote: I'll share that document with yourselves as well just so that you have a look at how we actually do the integrations from our side.

items.source: Avoma: SOW discussion and closure call, 2026-06-30

items.deliverable: Send sample World-Check One PDF reports for sanctions-screening integration scoping.
items.grounding_quote: I have about a dozen of them, I could share it in both of them.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin AI capabilities (Merlin Intake, Merlin Risk Radar, Merlin contract discovery) were demonstrated in depth and the buyer proactively asked to add Merlin Insights (AI spend analysis) into the SOW.

items.quote: Risk dashboard functionality must highlight supplier sanctions and geographic exposures instead of requiring manual research.
items.criterion: Risk dashboard flags supplier sanctions/geography without manual research

deltas.subject: The stage is recorded as Vendor Selected in Salesforce, but the deal was created 2 days ago with zero activity, zero stakeholders, and a 5-year-out close date. This stage may be incorrect or the deal may be a placeholder. If the stage is real, the absence of any post-selection activity (contracting, legal review, MSA negotiation) and the extreme close-date horizon suggest the selection is not yet operationalized.

deltas.to: Close date is 2031-07-01 (1,819 days out, 5 years). For a Vendor Selected deal with Best Case forecast, a 60-90 day close timeline is typical. The 5-year horizo
deltas.subject: Close date is 2031-07-01 (1,819 days out, 5 years). For a Vendor Selected deal with Best Case forecast, a 60-90 day close timeline is typical. The 5-year horizon is either a data-entry error or encodes a contract-end milestone rather than a signature target. This makes all velocity and urgency math meaningless.

deltas.to: No economic buyer, no executive sponsor, no CFO/procurement-director engagement is documented. Zero contact roles exist. For a $234k renewal at an enterprise ac
deltas.subject: No economic buyer, no executive sponsor, no CFO/procurement-director engagement is documented. Zero contact roles exist. For a $234k renewal at an enterprise account, exec alignment is required to secure budget and navigate procurement/legal, but it is completely unmapped.

deltas.to: No champion, no internal advocate, no buying-committee map exists. The deal is zero-threaded. Without a champion to drive the renewal, defend the budget, and na
deltas.subject: No champion, no internal advocate, no buying-committee map exists. The deal is zero-threaded. Without a champion to drive the renewal, defend the budget, and navigate internal approvals, the deal is at high risk of stalling or being deprioritized.

deltas.to: The known-topics note references detailed prior activity (material-breach negotiation resolved 17 Jun 2026, sanctions-screening scoping deferred to design phase
deltas.subject: The known-topics note references detailed prior activity (material-breach negotiation resolved 17 Jun 2026, sanctions-screening scoping deferred to design phase, implementation-partner ambiguity, legacy-ERP fragmentation scoping finalized 30 Jun 2026, and named stakeholders Marianne Ventura, Jacques de Villiers, Tracy Seeley-Jeppe, Dieter Sparbier, Zama Khambula, Ashen Chathury), but this opportunity (006P700000aX2mH) was created 2026-07-06 with zero calls or tasks. This suggests either (a) the known-topics history belongs to a DIFFERENT opportunity and was incorrectly associated with this one, or (b) this opp_id is a new placeholder for a prior engagement that was tracked elsewhere. Either way, the known-topics evidence is not traceable to this opp_id in the current sweep, creating a provenance gap.

Legacy ERP fragmentation (D365, AX2012 being phased out, QAD for Indonesia) forced Indonesia and DRC out of initial rollout scope; a supplier-master source-of-truth model (Zycus vs D365) was finalized 30 Jun 2026.

Lock in the change-management/partner-role workshop early in configuration kickoff to prevent the local-partner ambiguity (raised in both Oct 2025 and Jun 2026) from resurfacing as implementation risk.

value.detail: The known-topics note references detailed prior activity (material-breach negotiation resolved 17 Jun 2026, sanctions-screening scoping deferred to design phase, implementation-partner ambiguity, legacy-ERP fragmentation scoping finalized 30 Jun 2026, and named stakeholders Marianne Ventura, Jacques de Villiers, Tracy Seeley-Jeppe, Dieter Sparbier, Zama Khambula, Ashen Chathury), but this opportunity (006P700000aX2mH) was created 2026-07-06 with zero calls or tasks.
